question.text: On which day do monthly equity derivative contracts (options) expire on NSE?

answer.text: Last Tuesday of the expiry month (or previous trading day if Tuesday is a holiday)
Last Friday of the expiry month
Last Thursday of the expiry month
Last business day of the expiry month

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** On NSE, monthly equity derivative contracts expire on the LAST TUESDAY of the expiry month. If that Tuesday is a trading holiday, expiry shifts to the previous trading day.
**The Trap:** NSE changed from Thursday to Tuesday for monthly expiry. BSE uses Thursday. Students often confuse the two.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Expiry day is exchange-specific. NSE = Tuesday, BSE = Thursday. SEBI allows exchanges to choose.
**Key Takeaway:** NSE monthly expiry = last Tuesday. BSE monthly expiry = last Thursday. Holiday = previous trading day.
